incidents.canonical_name: Earthquake M5.9 Te Anau, New Zealand 2026-07-16
incidents.summary:
A magnitude 5.9 earthquake struck 42 km north-northwest of Te Anau, New Zealand, on July 16, 2026, initially reported as a 6.3 magnitude. The quake shook buildings across the South Island's tourist region and prompted a brief tsunami warning, which was later downgraded to a marine advisory. No injuries or damage were reported, and authorities lifted the tsunami alert shortly after.

A tsunami warning and immediate evacuation order were issued for parts of the South Island's west coast following the earthquake, but the alert has since been lifted. The mayor of the affected area reported property damage, and GeoNet recorded several lighter aftershocks after the main quake.

incidents.summary:
A powerful magnitude 7.3–7.4 earthquake struck off the coast of Mexico's southern Chiapas state on July 17, 2026, prompting a tsunami warning for nearby coasts of Mexico and Guatemala, with waves up to one meter possible. People evacuated buildings in affected areas, while authorities confirmed no tsunami threat to Hawaii.
A powerful earthquake of magnitude 7.3–7.4 struck off the coast of Chiapas, Mexico, near Puerto Madero on July 17, 2026, at a shallow depth of about 10 km. The quake triggered a tsunami warning and was strongly felt in Guatemala and El Salvador, prompting evacuations in some areas. No immediate damage or fatalities were reported, though a series of aftershocks were recorded.
New reports indicate that the 7.3-magnitude earthquake off the coast of Chiapas, Mexico, caused two injuries and cracked some buildings, though no major damage or fatalities were reported.
